task: I’m trying to put together a solid shortlist of the best hospitals in Texas for cardiac surgery for a family reference, so could you research this in a real browser and keep it grounded in actual hospital program pages and recognizable rankings? Start on Google and look for authoritative sources that would help identify strong Texas heart surgery centers, like U.S. News, Healthgrades, Leapfrog, CMS-related quality pages, or major hospital recognition pages, because I want the final list to be based on visible quality signals rather than guesswork. Then use U.S. News and those other quality indicators to narrow it down to the top 10 Texas hospitals for cardiac care or heart surgery. For each hospital you choose, open the actual cardiac surgery, heart surgery, or heart and vascular program page in its own tab and make sure the page clearly shows they offer advanced heart surgery services like CABG, valve repair or replacement, aortic surgery, or similar procedures. After that, create a CryptPad Sheets spreadsheet titled Top Texas Cardiac Hospitals and enter one row per hospital with the hospital name, city, a short description of the cardiac surgery program, whether it appears in rankings or quality indicators, and the direct link to the program page. Please leave all 10 hospital program tabs open so I can visually compare them later, and also keep the spreadsheet open in another tab. Once the sheet is filled out, add a short written summary in the sheet or a companion CryptPad Document explaining which Texas cities seem to have the strongest cardiac surgery centers based on how many top hospitals show up there and how prominent they are, so I can quickly see the main patterns and your top recommendations.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully identified and consulted authoritative sources, specifically U.S. News & World Report's cardiology and heart surgery rankings and Healthgrades, as evidenced by the search queries and webfetch actions (e.g., steps 4, 8, 10).

R2: failure - The agent identified 10 hospitals (Houston Methodist, UT Southwestern, Baylor St. Luke's, Baylor Scott & White Heart Hospital Plano, Houston Methodist The Woodlands, St. David's, Baylor Scott & White Heart Hospital Dallas, Memorial Hermann, Medical City Heart Hospital, and Methodist Healthcare San Antonio) based on the researched rankings. However, the agent failed to populate the spreadsheet with these records.

R3: success - The agent opened 10 distinct hospital program pages in separate tabs (step 42), which corresponds to the 10 hospitals identified.

R4: success - The agent verified advanced heart surgery services (CABG, valve repair, aortic surgery, etc.) for each hospital through webfetch actions (steps 31-40) before opening the tabs.

R5: failure - The agent failed to populate the spreadsheet with the required fields (hospital name, city, description, ranking, link). The spreadsheet remains empty in the final state.

R6: failure - The agent created a CryptPad sheet, but it was never populated with the 10 hospital records. The spreadsheet remains blank.

R7: failure - The agent failed to provide the required written summary and did not populate the spreadsheet. While the tabs and spreadsheet were left open, the content requirements were not met.

rubric_results.requirement: Authoritative ranking or quality indicator sources relevant to Texas cardiac care hospitals are identified and used as the basis for selection.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm from browser history, open tabs, or notes that Google results and recognized sources such as U.S. News, Healthgrades, Leapfrog, CMS-related pages, or comparable quality sources were consulted.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully identified and consulted authoritative sources, specifically U.S. News & World Report's cardiology and heart surgery rankings and Healthgrades, as evidenced by the search queries and webfetch actions (e.g., steps 4, 8, 10).

rubric_results.requirement: A final set of exactly 10 Texas hospitals is selected based on visible ranking presence or quality indicators for cardiac care or heart surgery.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can count exactly 10 hospitals in the final sheet and see that each one has some ranking or quality-indicator notation tied to the researched sources.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent identified 10 hospitals (Houston Methodist, UT Southwestern, Baylor St. Luke's, Baylor Scott & White Heart Hospital Plano, Houston Methodist The Woodlands, St. David's, Baylor Scott & White Heart Hospital Dallas, Memorial Hermann, Medical City Heart Hospital, and Methodist Healthcare San Antonio) based on the researched rankings. However, the agent failed to populate the spreadsheet with these records.

rubric_results.requirement: Each selected hospital has its cardiac surgery, heart surgery, or heart and vascular program page opened in a separate browser tab.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can visually confirm 10 distinct hospital program tabs are open, each corresponding to one hospital listed in the spreadsheet.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent opened 10 distinct hospital program pages in separate tabs (step 42), which corresponds to the 10 hospitals identified.

rubric_results.requirement: For each selected hospital, the agent verifies that advanced heart surgery services are offered.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can inspect the open hospital pages and see explicit references to advanced cardiac surgery services such as CABG, valve procedures, aortic surgery, or equivalent surgical offerings.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent verified advanced heart surgery services (CABG, valve repair, aortic surgery, etc.) for each hospital through webfetch actions (steps 31-40) before opening the tabs.

rubric_results.requirement: For each of the 10 hospitals, the required fields are accurately captured: hospital name, city, cardiac program description, ranking or quality appearance, and program page link.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can compare the spreadsheet rows against the open hospital tabs and ranking sources to confirm all five fields are present and consistent for all 10 entries.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent failed to populate the spreadsheet with the required fields (hospital name, city, description, ranking, link). The spreadsheet remains empty in the final state.

rubric_results.requirement: A CryptPad Sheets spreadsheet titled 'Top Texas Cardiac Hospitals' is created and populated with the 10 hospital records.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can see an open CryptPad Sheets tab with the exact title and a structured table containing 10 rows of hospital data.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent created a CryptPad sheet, but it was never populated with the 10 hospital records. The spreadsheet remains blank.

rubric_results.requirement: The final output includes a concise summary identifying which Texas cities appear to have the strongest cardiac surgery centers and the spreadsheet and hospital tabs remain open for visual review.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can see the summary text in the sheet or companion CryptPad Document and confirm the spreadsheet tab plus the hospital program tabs are still open.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent failed to provide the required written summary and did not populate the spreadsheet. While the tabs and spreadsheet were left open, the content requirements were not met.
